market_context:
Cohort size: 201 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £154.60
Median 90-day occupancy: 47.8%

analysis.rewritten_desc: Experience a premium stay in Birmingham’s vibrant NEC district at this spacious, entire‑home retreat. Located just minutes from the NEC, the Belfry and Drayton Manor, the property comfortably accommodates up to 4 guests, offering a tranquil escape for families or small groups. Guests enjoy a high rating of 4.87★ from 15 reviews, reflecting consistent satisfaction with cleanliness, comfort and location. The home is priced at £791.40 per night, a luxury offering that stands out in the market. While the current 90‑day calendar shows 31.5% occupancy (62 open nights), there is strong potential to boost bookings by aligning the price and value proposition with guest expectations. Although no specific amenities are listed, the property’s proximity to major event venues, shopping and dining makes it an ideal base for business travellers and leisure visitors alike. With a Superhost status yet to be earned, the host, Jacquelyne, is eager to enhance the listing and deliver an exceptional experience. Book now to secure a high‑end Birmingham stay that combines convenience, comfort and a prime location for your next visit.
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a high‑quality exterior shot showing the house’s frontage and nearby parking as the cover photo – it instantly signals convenience and the premium nature of the property.
key_diagnosis: Your nightly rate of £791.4 is 412% higher than the city median (£154.6) while your 90‑day occupancy is only 31.5% (vs. median 47.8%) – price‑value mismatch is driving low bookings.
The title claims the home sleeps 10, but the listed capacity is 4 guests; correcting this will prevent guest disappointment and improve conversion.
No amenities are listed; adding high‑value amenities such as outdoor space, parking and entertainment can attract more bookings and justify the premium rate.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your nightly rate of £791.4 is 412% above the Birmingham median, yet your 90‑day occupancy sits at just 31.5% – there’s a clear upside.
